strengths: Three distinct, specific real-world objects are clearly identified and contextualized (Wikipedia page on the Dancing Plague, British Library entrance, Pioneer 10 plaque).
Each object is explicitly linked to the IA prompt âWho owns knowledge?â through TOK concepts such as access, custodianship, and authority.
Several annotations highlight precise description of context and effective illustration of artefacts (e.g. the British Library entrance, the Pioneer 10 plaque image).
weaknesses: Explanations remain largely descriptive and repetitive, often reducing arguments to âaccess equals ownershipâ without depth.
Claims about gatekeeping, reliability, and institutional control are asserted without supporting evidence, policy examples, or scholarly references.
Analysis drifts away from sustained, prompt-focused justification; alternative perspectives and counterarguments are not explored, weakening the critical engagement.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition demonstrates clear identification of three specific objects and their real-world contexts, and makes explicit links to the IA prompt, satisfying the requirements for band 3â4. However, the analysis lacks the depth, precision, and evidential support characteristic of higher bands. Arguments are descriptive and repetitive, evidence and alternative viewpoints are absent, and connections to TOK concepts are not fully developed. These strengths and weaknesses align precisely with the descriptors for a band 3â4 performance, justifying a final score of 4.

annotations.comment: The analysis of mass hysteria and knowledge ownership here is largely descriptive and repeats the idea that âaccess equals ownershipâ without supporting evidence or reference to scholarly sources on Wikipediaâs editorial processes.

The justification that limited information âquestions the validity and reliabilityâ is asserted without citing any specific policy or epistemological framework, weakening the connection to the prompt about ownership of knowledge.
The concluding claim that NASA âshapes knowledgeâ is interesting but lacks depth. Cite specific instances (e.g., data release schedules) to illustrate how NASA controls dissemination.
The IA prompt âWho owns knowledge?â is presented but not explicitly revisited in the following paragraphs. Anchor each justification back to the prompt to clarify the argument.
The student offers a precise description of the British Libraryâs entrance and scope, effectively establishing the objectâs real-world context and its relevance to information access.

The conclusion that âownership of knowledge implies knowing broader background informationâ is a strong claim but remains unsupported by concrete examples of how deeper context changes interpretation.

Using a generic Wikipedia screenshot limits the authenticity of the object. Consider sourcing a unique artifact or discussing specific revision histories to deepen the link to ownership and custodianship.

The student clearly identifies the first objectâa specific Simple English Wikipedia page on the Dancing Plague of 1518âand situates it in its real-world historical context, fulfilling the requirement to link an explicit object to the IA prompt.
Sentence structure and flow issues (e.g., mid-paragraph breaks) disrupt reader comprehension. Edit for coherence so arguments about ownership follow logically.
The historical account of branches dating back to the 17th century is vague. Specify which collections or dates to strengthen the context and support the narrative of evolving custodianship.
Claims about gated access and digital versions lack citation of specific library policies. Reference actual access regulations or reader pass guidelines to substantiate these points.
The notion that pass holders âownâ the knowledge they access conflates temporary permission with epistemic ownership. Explore counterarguments about communal stewardship to deepen the analysis.
While the photograph situates the library visually, the student could discuss how architectural design or signage contributes to perceptions of authority and ownership.
The student explicitly identifies the Pioneer 10 plaque and its launch context, successfully grounding the object in a clear real-world scenario.
Assertions about NASAâs sole ownership versus collective humanity are stated without reference to treaties (e.g., Outer Space Treaty) or policies governing space exploration.
This section begins to pose a question about ownership but is truncated (âIs it solelyâ¦â). Ensure full, coherent sentences to maintain clarity of argument.
The list of stakeholders is promising but offered in passing. Develop each stakeholderâs perspective with evidence or examples of how they influence the ownership debate.
Referencing the â12 conceptsâ feels generic and distracts from the focus on the IA prompt. Tie conceptual terminology directly to how they illuminate questions of ownership.
The inclusion of the Pioneer 10 plaque image is highly effective, illustrating the physical artifact whose knowledge-bearing intentions are central to your TOK discussion.
The bibliography header is present, but there is no commentary on how these sources were selected or their relevance to your TOK analysis, missing an opportunity for reflection.
References are limited to URLs without formal citation details (authors, dates). Use a consistent referencing style to strengthen academic rigor.
Consider expanding your bibliography to include policy documents, scholarly articles on knowledge ownership, and space treaties to support your claims more robustly.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
